name: Green Bean, Red Onion, and Roast Potato Salad
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary leaves, or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
1 red onion, halved lengthwise and sliced thin lengthwise
2 pounds green beans, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
24 kalamata or niçoise olives, pitted and halved
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the potatoes (unpeeled) and cut them into 1-inch wedges.
2. In a large roasting pan, heat 1/3 cup of oil in the center of a preheated 425°F oven for 5 minutes.
3. Add the potatoes to the hot oil, tossing to coat with oil, and roast for 30 minutes or until tender.
4. Let the potatoes cool in the pan.
5. In a blender, puree garlic, vinegar, rosemary leaves, and salt to taste. With the motor running, add the remaining 1/3 cup oil in a stream. Blend until emulsified.
6. Place onion halves in ice-cold water for 5 minutes, drain well, and pat dry.
7. Boil green beans in boiling salted water for 5 minutes or until crisp-tender, then drain in a colander.
8. Refresh beans under cold water and pat dry.
9. In a very large bowl combine potatoes, onion, green beans, and olives.
10. Add dressing and toss gently.
11. Serve salad garnished with rosemary sprigs at room temperature. Serves 8 to 10.
